question.text: At the time of normal retirement from NPS Tier 1 at age 60, what is the maximum percentage of the accumulated corpus that can be withdrawn as a tax-free lump sum?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At normal retirement (age 60), an NPS Tier 1 subscriber can withdraw up to 60% of the accumulated corpus as a tax-free lump sum. The remaining 40% must be used to purchase an annuity from an empanelled insurance company, and the annuity income received is taxable as per the subscriber's income tax slab. **The Trap:** 40% is the mandatory annuity portion, not the lump sum -- candidates who remember the number but mix up its application will select this. 80% is the annuity requirement for premature exit (before 60), not normal retirement. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers NPS exit rules; NISM Series V-A covers retirement planning products including NPS. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help clients plan for the mandatory 40% annuity well before retirement. The annuity rate locked in at retirement determines lifelong income -- in a low interest rate environment, this could mean significantly lower monthly pension. Consider timing of exit strategically.
